LAW AND MOTION 302, PLAINTIFF RICHARD DENTON'S MOTION FOR RECONSIDERATION OF ORDER RE MOTION TO STRIKE DEFENDANTS HALE AND MITCHELL FROM THE FEHA CLAIM IN PLAINTIFFS SEVENTH CAUSE OF ACTION. PLAINTIFF RICHARD DENTON'S MOTION FOR RECONSIDERATION OF THE COURT'S ORAL RULING STRIKING DEFENDANT BARBARA HALE FROM THE SEVENTH CAUSE OF ACTION FOR UNLAWFUL HARASSMENT IN HIS SECOND AMENDED COMPLAINT IS DENIED. THE COURT WILL ADDRESS THE MERITS OF THIS MOTION BASED ON THE SEPTEMBER 25, 2018 HEARING SINCE NO WRITTEN ORDER HAS YET BEEN FILED AS TO THE RULING SOUGHT TO BE RECONSIDERED. THE EEOC DOCUMENTS ARE NOT NEW FACTS THAT WARRANT RECONSIDERATION. THE COURT ALREADY HAD THOSE DOCUMENTS WHEN IT MADE ITS PRIOR RULING. MOST SIGNIFICANTLY, COLE V. ANTELOPE VALLEY UNION HIGH SCHOOL DIST. (1996) 47 CAL.APP.4TH 1505, 1515 IS ON POINT AUTHORITY THAT MS. HALE NEEDED TO BE NAMED IN THE CAPTION OR BODY OF THE ADMINISTRATIVE CHARGE FOR MR. DENTON TO PURSUE HER IN THIS ACTION. NOR IS MR. DENTON'S FILING OF AN ADDITIONAL ADMINISTRATIVE CHARGE AFTER THE SEPTEMBER 25 HEARING A NEW FACT OR CIRCUMSTANCE THAT WARRANTS RECONSIDERATION. HOWEVER, THE ORAL RULING GIVING MR. DENTON LEAVE TO AMEND REMAINS. COUNSEL FOR DEFENDANTS IS REQUIRED TO PREPARE A PROPOSED ORDER WHICH REPEATS VERBATIM THE SUBSTANTIVE PORTIONS OF THE COURT'S RULING IN COMPLIANCE WITH CRC 3.1312. JUDGE: HAROLD KAHN, CLERK: W. TRUPEK, COURT REPORTER: MARIA TORREANO, CSR#8600, MARIA.TORREANO@GMAIL.COM., NOT REPORTED. =(302/HK)

Notice Of Revised Renewed Motion And Revised Renewed Motion To Reconsider The Order Voiding The Notice Of Settlement
Off Calendar May-21-2019 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: PLAINTIFF RICHARD DENTON'S REVISED RENEWED MOTION TO RECONSIDER THE ORDER VOIDING THE NOTICE OF SETTLEMENT

No appearances. The Court adopts its tentative ruling as follows: Plaintiff Richard Denton's "revised renewed motion to reconsider" is denied. Even assuming plaintiff's characterization of the appellate record, I cannot, for several reasons, grant the relief sought - enforcement of what plaintiff calls the "settlement agreement."

First, a settlement would require approval by the Board of Supervisors and the Public Utilities Commission, but the record is bare of evidence that either approval occurred.

Second, the settlement cannot be enforced under CCP 664.6 because that statute requires direct party participation. The parties must sign a written stipulation for entry of judgment or the parties must enter into a stipulation "orally before the court." (CCP 664.6; Levy v. Sup. Ct. (1995) 10 Cal.4th 578, 584.) Neither occurred here.

Third, plaintiff seeks a settlement that would only resolve the case through September 18, 2015, and would not dispose of the entire case. But the City has not agreed to a piecemeal settlement.

Fourth, plaintiff seeks all his attorney fees to enforce the settlement, litigate the appeal, etc., but "[c]onsent is not mutual, unless the parties all agree upon the same thing in the same sense." (Civ. Code 1580.) The City never agreed to pay the attorney fees plaintiff seeks.

Fifth, there is no evidence the purported settlement was ratified; plaintiff fails to show that the City received an advantage from it. Order signed. Judge: Richard B. Ulmer, Clerk: E. Larnauti, Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR 8600 (Email: maria.torreano@gmail.com) (302/RBU)

Notice Of Motion And Motion For Reconsideration Of Order Re Motion To Strike Defendants Hale And Mitchell From The Feha Claim In Plaintiffs Seventh Cause Of Action
Off Calendar Dec-06-2018 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
No appearances. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

PLAINTIFF RICHARD DENTON'S Motion For Reconsideration Of Order Re Motion To Strike Defendants Hale And Mitchell From The Feha Claim In Plaintiffs Seventh Cause Of Action

Plaintiff Richard Denton's motion for reconsideration of the court's oral ruling striking defendant Barbara Hale from the seventh cause of action for unlawful harassment in his second amended complaint is denied. The court will address the merits of this motion based on the September 25, 2018 hearing since no written order has yet been filed as to the ruling sought to be reconsidered. The EEOC documents are not new facts that warrant reconsideration. The court already had those documents when it made its prior ruling. Most significantly, Cole v. Antelope Valley Union High School Dist. (1996) 47 Cal.App.4th 1505, 1515 is on point authority that Ms. Hale needed to be named in the caption or body of the administrative charge for Mr. Denton to pursue her in this action. Nor is Mr. Denton's filing of an additional administrative charge after the September 25 hearing a new fact or circumstance that warrants reconsideration. However, the oral ruling giving Mr. Denton leave to amend remains.

Counsel for defendants is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portions of the Court's ruling in compliance with CRC 3.1312.

Off Calendar Aug-10-2018 Continued To Oct-17-18 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

MOTION TO STRIKE 2ND AMENDED COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Aug-28-2018 Continued To Sep-25-18 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 302
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances: Plaintiff RICHARD DENTON (Tel: 619-817-5760) appearing in pro per;
Atty. LISA B. BERKOWITZ, ESQ. of the City Attorney's Office of the City and County of San Francisco (Tel: 415-554-3825) appearing for defendants City and County of San Francisco, Barbara Hale, John Doyle and Lori Mitchell.

DEFENDANTS CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO, BARBARA HALE, JOHN DOYLE, AND LORI MITCHELL'S MOTION TO STRIKE PORTIONS OF THE SECOND AMENDED COMPLAINT FILED BY PLAINTIFF RICHARD DENTON

The matter is argued. The Court adopts its tentative ruling in part but defers its ruling as to the seventh cause of action as to defendants Barbara Hale and Lori Mitchell only. The Court permits supplemental briefing by those defendants on the issue of whether failure to name a party in administrative charge precludes the FEA claim. This supplemental briefing is due 9/5/18. Plaintiff's reply brief is due 9/10/18. Today's hearing is continued to 9/25/18 at 9:30 a.m. in Dept. 302 for further hearing on that one issue. The Court otherwise adopts its tentative ruling and rules today as follows: Defendants City and County of San Francisco, Barbara Hale, John Doyle, and Lori Mitchell's motion to strike portions of the second amended complaint filed by plaintiff Richard Denton is: a) granted with 20 days leave to amend as to paragraph 62; b) granted without leave to amend as to the allegations in the eighth cause of action for failure to prevent discrimination against the individual defendants; and 3) denied as to all other portions of the second amended complaint sought to be stricken (except for the aforementioned issue for which the court is defering ruling to 9/25/18). Paragraphs 57-61 and 63-66 raise allegations similar to the allegations in the DFEH/EEOC charge. Per Okoli v. Lockheed Technical Operations Co. (1995) 36 Cal.App.4th 1607, Mr. Denton does not need to return to the DFEH every time there is a new incident of discrimination that is similar to what has previously occurred. Paragraph 62 refers to an accommodation request by Mr. Denton following his surgery. Mr. Denton has leave to clarify that the request was related to the medical condition referred to in the DFEH/EEOC charge. The harassment allegations withstand a motion to strike for the same reason that the harassment cause of action withstands defendants' demurrer. The failure to prevent discrimination allegations alleged against Barbara Hale, John Doyle, and Lori Mitchell are stricken because a failure to prevent discrimination claim can only be asserted against an employer. Defendants have withdrawn the portion of their motion seeking to strike defamation allegations. The Court instructs parties to hold off on submitting a proposed order until the Court has first ruled on the issue that has been deferred to 9/25/18.

Judge: Harold Kahn, Clerk: Sean Kane, Court Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR#8600, torreanom@aol.com, Reported. (302/HK)

DEMURRER TO 2ND AMENDED COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Aug-28-2018 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances: Plaintiff RICHARD DENTON (Tel: 619-817-5760) appearing in pro per;
Atty. LISA B. BERKOWITZ, ESQ. of the City Attorney's Office of the City and County of San Francisco (Tel: 415-554-3825) appearing for defendants City and County of San Francisco, Barbara Hale, John Doyle and Lori Mitchell.

DEFENDANTS CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO, BARBARA HALE, JOHN DOYLE AND LORI MITCHELL's DEMURRER TO SECOND AMENDED COMPLAINT

The matter is argued and submitted by the parties. The Court adopts its tentative ruling. Defendants City and County of San Francisco, Barbara Hale, John Doyle, and Lori Mitchell's demurrer to the sixth cause of action for violation of the Medical Information Act and the seventh cause of action for harassment in the second amended complaint filed by plaintiff Richard Denton is sustained with 20 days leave to amend as to the sixth cause of action and overruled as to the seventh cause of action. The request for judicial notice of the April 16, 2013 government claim is granted. On its face that claim suggests that Mr. Denton's CMIA cause of action accrued in July of 2012 and Mr. Denton filed an untimely government claim more than six months later. Mr. Denton is given leave to amend to allege specific facts to support application of the discovery rule to render his government claim timely. "In order to rely on the discovery rule for delayed accrual of a cause of action, '[a] plaintiff whose complaint shows on its face that his claim would be barred without the benefit of the discovery rule must specifically plead facts to show (1) the time and manner of discovery and (2) the inability to have made earlier discovery despite reasonable diligence.'"(Fox v. Ethicon Endo-Surgery, Inc. (2005) 35 Cal.4th 797, 808(citation omitted.)). Defendants do not cite any authority that Mr. Denton needed to seek leave to present a late claim. Rason v. Santa Barbara City Housing Authority (1988) 201 Cal.App.3d 817, 822 indicates otherwise. The scope of Mr. Denton's permissible causes of action is limited not by the March 12, 2013 DFEH/EEOC charge, but by the scope of the DFEH/EEOC investigation which can reasonably be expected to grow out of the charge. Mr. Denton's harassment cause of action is "like or reasonably related to" the claims that were expressly stated in the charge. The harassment cause of action is viable because it would reasonably have been uncovered in an investigation of the charges that were made. (Okoli v. Lockheed Technical Operations Co. (1995) 36 Cal.App.4th 1607.). The Court signs a formal order.
